title: Spectral Light Restoration
description: A broken stained-glass window floats in a dark cathedral space, illuminated by a magical lantern. The user manipulates custom shaders on the lantern to emit different spectral frequencies, each frequency restoring a different color channel of the shattered glass. Players must balance RGB energy levels, undo misallocations, and couple multiple shader uniform inputs to fully restore the window's luminous beauty.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 that loads Lantern.glb and GlassBrokenWindow.glb from shared_assets/. Place the broken glass window model centered at (0,2,0) facing the camera. Place the lantern at (0,0.5,2) in front of the window. Use a dark environment with minimal ambient light (intensity 0.05) to simulate a cathedral at night. Camera at (0,2,5) looking at the window. Apply a custom ShaderMaterial to the glass window with uniforms: uRedEnergy, uGreenEnergy, uBlueEnergy (all floats 0-1, starting at 0). The fragment shader should multiply each color channel of the base texture by its corresponding energy uniform, so the glass appears black initially and progressively reveals color. Apply a custom ShaderMaterial to the lantern with a glow effect using a uniform uSpectralMode (int 0=red,1=green,2=blue) that tints the emissive glow accordingly, and uIntensity (float 0-1). Create an HTML overlay panel with: three vertical sliders for R/G/B energy allocation (0-100), a spectral mode toggle button cycling red/green/blue, an intensity knob slider, and Undo/Redo buttons. Implement a conservation law: total energy across R+G+B cannot exceed 150 units, so increasing one slider must auto-reduce others proportionally. Each slider change counts as an interaction step. The spectral mode toggle changes which channel the lantern visually glows and applies a 1.2x multiplier bonus to that channel's shader uniform. Maintain a history stack of all state changes (slider values plus mode) for undo/redo with at least 14 levels. Add a PointLight parented to the lantern whose color matches uSpectralMode and intensity matches uIntensity. When all three energy channels reach above 0.8 simultaneously, trigger a completion effect: the glass window shader plays a ripple distortion animation using a time uniform and the lantern pulses brightly. Display a small text HUD showing current energy percentages and total budget remaining.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  redEnergy: number,         // red channel energy level (0-1)
  greenEnergy: number,         // green channel energy level (0-1)
  blueEnergy: number,         // blue channel energy level (0-1)
  spectralMode: number,         // lantern spectral mode (0=red, 1=green, 2=blue)
  lanternIntensity: number,         // lantern glow intensity (0-1)
  totalEnergyUnits: number,         // sum of RGB energy in units (0-150)
  glassOpacity: number,         // overall glass visibility (0-1)
  historyStackSize: number,         // number of undo states available
  isEnergyBalanced: boolean,         // whether RGB energies sum to exactly 150 units
  dominantChannel: string,         // which color channel has highest energy (red|green|blue)
  restorationProgress: number,         // percentage of window restoration (0-100)
}
